question_text: Let {рќ‘‹аЇЎ}, рќ‘›в‰Ґ1, be a sequence of random variables with the probability mass functions рќ‘ќаЇ‘аЇЎ(рќ‘Ґ) = рќ‘›+ 1 , рќ‘Ґ= 0, рќ‘›+ 1 , рќ‘Ґ= рќ‘›, 0, elsewhere. Let рќ‘‹ be a random variable with рќ‘ѓ(рќ‘‹= 0) = 1. Then which of the following statements is/are true?

options.A: рќ‘‹аЇЎ converges to рќ‘‹ in distribution
options.B: рќ‘‹аЇЎ converges to рќ‘‹ in probability
options.D: There exists a subsequence {рќ‘‹аЇЎаі–} of {рќ‘‹аЇЎ} such that рќ‘‹аЇЎаі– converges to рќ‘‹ almost surely Page 34
